heroDescription: ByteDance Seedream generates and edits images for text-to-image, image-to-image, and high-quality visual creation workflows.
seoDescription: Compare Seedream 5.0 Lite and Seedream 4.5 image models on Novita AI for text-to-image and image-to-image workflows.
overviewBody: Seedream is ByteDance Seed's image generation model family. Public Seedream releases cover bilingual text-to-image generation, unified image generation and editing, multi-image composition, and high-resolution creative output.
Novita AI exposes Seedream models for image generation and editing workflows where teams need prompt-following output, image-to-image iteration, or efficient visual creation.
Use this collection to compare Seedream 5.0 Lite and Seedream 4.5, then open a model page for API behavior, pricing, and Playground testing.

lineupSections.title: Seedream 5.0 Lite
lineupSections.description: Seedream 5.0 Lite is a newer lightweight Seedream release for efficient image generation workflows.
items: Use it for newer Seedream image generation workflows where a lightweight latest-generation model is preferred.
Official material emphasizes improved prompt following and alignment; exact Novita parameter support should be confirmed on the model page.

lineupSections.title: Seedream 4.x
lineupSections.description: Seedream 4.x models are used for text-to-image and image-to-image visual creation workflows.
items: Use Seedream 4.5 for enabled Novita image generation and editing workflows.
Public official documentation is stronger for Seedream 4.0 than 4.5-specific behavior, so confirm model-page parameters before integration.

faq.question: What is Seedream used for?
faq.answer: Seedream is used for AI image generation and editing, including text-to-image and image-to-image workflows.

faq.question: Which Seedream models are available on Novita AI?
faq.answer: The Seedream collection includes Seedream 5.0 Lite and Seedream 4.5.

faq.question: When should I use Seedream 5.0 Lite versus Seedream 4.5?
faq.answer: Start with Seedream 5.0 Lite for a newer lightweight option, and compare Seedream 4.5 when you need to evaluate output style or behavior against an earlier enabled version.
